overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, with a combined following of 3,580. His content focuses on computational social science, disinformation research, and academic opportunities. The audience is highly engaged, particularly on Bluesky where discussions around coordinated inauthentic behavior generate significant amplification through reposts, while LinkedIn remains a key hub for professional announcements and hiring.

contentThemes.description: Updates on software tools like CooRnet, discussions on platform methodology, and research papers targeting network analysis of digital manipulation.
Hiring posts for postdoc positions, callouts for PhD opportunities, and promoting research grants.
Sharing newly published papers and promoting upcoming conference presentations like DisinfoConf2025.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on Bluesky to leverage its highly responsive audience and strong reposting behavior for research updates.
Utilize rich media, such as infographics or visual network charts, on LinkedIn to boost engagement on high-value posts like hiring and grant announcements.
Synchronize the launch of software releases like CooRnet across all three platforms with tailored messaging to drive higher cross-platform traffic.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is the most engaging platform with an average engagement rate of 6.14%, driven by high repost counts. Academic calls and paper announcements resonate extremely well with this active, community-driven crowd.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon yields steady engagement (4.55% average) but lower reach. It serves as a solid hub for technical and methodological discussions among decentralized academic peers.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest audience (1,850 followers) but a slightly lower relative average engagement rate (4.87%). It remains highly effective for formal announcements, such as research grants and job recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
